backtest.headline: Within 0.004 of the Vegas closing line
backtest.summary: We scored our in-season game model head-to-head against the real Vegas closing moneyline on every game of the 2025-26 season -- the sharpest public number there is. Over 1,083 games we land within four thousandths of a nat of the market on log-loss. Almost no public model reaches the closing line; we sit a half-step behind it (a naive Elo baseline runs ≈.693 log-loss on the same games; we land at .6859 vs the close's .6822).
backtest.note: The market's small, consistent edge is the real-time information we don't yet ingest -- confirmed starting goalie, late scratches, sharp money. Closing that gap (a game-time starter + injury feed) is the next frontier, not a modeling flaw.

liveNote: Live tier: predictions LOCKED before puck drop and graded against the result — a record that provably existed before the games. Begins opening night 2026-27; empty over the summer because the warehouse holds no unplayed games. The backtest above is the same model scored on 2025-26, honestly retrodictive. Vegas stays in the backtest — the closing line is a stored snapshot, not an in-season feed.
